jobs.title: Managing Director, Mergers & Acquisitions
jobs.description:
In a world of disruption and increasingly complex business challenges, our professionals bring truth into focus with the Kroll Lens. Our sharp analytical skills, paired with the latest technology, allow us to give our clients clarity—not just answers—in all areas of business. We value the diverse backgrounds and perspectives that enable us to think globally. As part of One team, One Kroll , you’ll contribute to a supportive and collaborative work environment that empowers you to excel.

Kroll’s M&A Advisory service line is a leading middle-market M&A advisor. We advise public corporations, financial sponsors, family-owned businesses and other private companies in middle-market buy-side and sell-side M&A transactions globally, with regional teams situated in U.S., Canada, UK, Germany, Ireland, France, China, Brazil and India. Kroll has extensive experience in buy-side and sell-side engagements, capital raising, transaction advisory services and financial sponsor coverage.

At Kroll, your work will help deliver clarity to our clients’ most complex governance, risk and transparency challenges. Apply now to join One team, One Kroll .

Responsibilities

We have developed our expertise through extensive work in sell-side, buy-side and divestiture assignments on behalf of portfolio companies of private equity funds, closely held businesses and publicly traded companies.

Work on a variety of transactions in all stages, from initial client pitches to transaction closings
Communicates regulatory, financial information and complex strategic issues in a clear, concise and relevant manner to assist and guide decision-makers in corporate finance transactions
Ability to market services both to internal partners and external clients, develop client networks, and support efforts in developing and executing sales and marketing strategies
Ensure quality of client deliverables by having a strong attention to detail
Mentor and develop Staff, Vice Presidents and Directors
Individuals will be assigned a wide variety of projects and given as much responsibility as their experience and capabilities permit

Requirements

Minimum 10 years’ experience at a middle market, reputable boutique or regional investment bank serving the middle market with demonstrable M&A execution experience.
Bachelor's, Master's or MBA degree
Ability to generate new business through the cultivation of existing relationships as well as the development of new relationships by various efforts including cold calling.
Ability to cross-sell other firm services within Corporate Finance and our other business units of GRA, Cyber, VAS and Kroll Business Services.
Ability to make effective decisions by analyzing information and considering priorities
Proficient in MS Office, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
Demonstrated experience with managing of day to day aspects of client relationships and projects
Demonstrated record of leadership and effective management in matrixed organizations
Excellent written and verbal communication skills that help represent diverse communities
Experience working with diverse teams

In order to be considered for a position, you must formally apply via careers.kroll.com.

Kroll is committed to creating an inclusive work environment. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and will consider all qualified applicants regardless of gender, gender identity, race, religion, color, nationality, ethnic origin, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, age or disability.

The Opportunity

As a Thought Leadership Survey Development Director, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of industries by creating unique perspectives and insights on overarching themes and industry-specific subjects. Within our Internal Firm Services practice, you will drive strategic sales and marketing initiatives, enhancing brand visibility and capturing new business opportunities. By utilizing market research, digital marketing, and creative campaigns, you will engage clients and promote the firm's services.

As a Director, you will set the strategic direction and lead business growth efforts. You will make impactful decisions and oversee multiple projects, maintaining executive-level relations. Translating the vision, you will set the tone and inspire others to follow. Your role is crucial in driving business growth, shaping the direction of projects, and mentoring the next generation of leaders. You are expected to be a guardian of PwC's reputation, understanding that quality, integrity, inclusion, and a commercial mindset are all foundational to our success.

In this role, you will develop new ideas, solutions, and structures, driving thought leadership. You will solve problems by exploring multiple angles and using creativity, encouraging others to do the same. You will also promote technological advances, creating an environment where people and technology thrive together.

Responsibilities

Developing and executing strategic sales and marketing initiatives to drive revenue growth and enhance brand visibility
Creating unique perspectives and insights on industry-specific subjects to position PwC as trusted advisors
Collaborating with teams to frame distinctive points of view that shape the future of industries
Utilizing market research and digital marketing strategies to capture new business opportunities
Promoting the Firm's services through creative campaigns and effective sales strategies
Conducting research and analysis to inform content creation and development
Leading thought leadership efforts by developing new ideas, solutions, and structures
Managing content quality and optimization to validate impactful communication
Identifying gaps in the market and converting opportunities into success for the Firm
Cultivating potential in others and mentoring the next generation of leaders

What You Must Have

At least a High School Diploma or the equivalent degree
At least 8 years of experience in sales, marketing or PwC experience

What Sets You Apart

Preference for a Bachelor's degree
Demonstrating strategic leadership in thought leadership initiatives
Excelling in content creation and content strategy
Utilizing data analytics for market insights and decision-making
Leading survey design and survey research projects
Promoting innovation and creativity in content development
Cultivating potential through coaching and feedback

The salary range for this position is: $122,500 - $423,780. For residents of Washington state the salary range for this position is: $122,500 - $504,500. Actual compensation within the range will be dependent upon the individual's skills, experience, qualifications and location, and applicable employment laws. All hired individuals are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus. jobs.title: CEO Communications Manager
jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.
Title:
CEO Communications Manager
San Francisco (in-office required) 
The CEO Communications Manager opportunity

We’re seeking a high-performing CEO Communications Manager to help amplify the voice and vision of our CEO and drive AI optimization across his communications channels. In this role, you will report to Okta’s Head of CEO Communications and partner closely with the Office of the CEO to support, streamline, and scale the CEO comms platform, spanning internal and external speaking events, media & influencer engagements, and social media. You will also work with the Corporate Communications team and partner with the broader executive bench, cross-functional partners, and our various agencies to develop bylines, blog posts, keynotes, talking points, and thought leadership content.
This is a rare opportunity to sit at the intersection of content, operations, strategy, and execution—all in support of a visionary founder leading a global tech company through transformation and growth. The ideal candidate is intellectually curious, highly accountable, and capable of grasping both business strategy as well as the underlying technology, particularly in the context of identity security, AI, and enterprise software. 
What you’ll be doing 

Content development:
Develop and refine bylines, scripts, talking points, social copy, and briefing materials for internal and external engagements that faithfully reflect the CEO’s vision and voice across audiences ranging from employees and customers to global media and boards of directors.
Data driven optimization:
Establish and maintain rigorous feedback loops and use engagement, sentiment, and reach data to continuously refine the CEO’s communication strategy, with an eye toward emerging trends in identity, AI, and enterprise tech.
Agentic innovation:
Continuously
apply and explore how AI and agents can securely optimize and automate workflows and content development across the CEO’s communications platform.
Event planning & production support:
Serve as point of contact for production logistics across company events, including tech run-throughs, AV coordination, studio operations, and real-time event support.
Stakeholder management:
Design and own streamlined, scalable workflows for the end-to-end content lifecycle—from ideation and drafting through cross-functional, legal, and PR review, to distribution. Manage inputs, timelines, and deliverables across multiple stakeholders, often coordinating between executives and production/design teams to ensure alignment across all CEO materials.
Operational excellence:
The ideal candidate will thrive in and derive energy from a high-pressure, high-performing environment. Obsessive quality control and meticulous attention to detail are table stakes. You will  
anticipate needs, respond quickly, and act as a strategic thought partner—particularly in high-stakes, fast-paced settings. A bias for optimization and innovation will be highly valued.

What you’ll bring to the role & what we’re looking for

5-7+ years of experience in corporate communications including direct experience with executive and/or CEO communications, ideally in a high-growth tech or SaaS company
Experience writing with and for CEOs, executives, leaders, and/or founders, with the ability to adapt to and maintain a distinct executive tone of voice
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, with high attention to detail
Experience with and eagerness to apply AI to enhance and reimagine processes while improving content quality and speed
A passion for operational excellence and ability to thrive in a high-performance environment, taking initiative, owning outcomes, and operating with polish and discretion
Strong project management instincts—capable of juggling shifting priorities, tight timelines, and multiple stakeholders with calm and precision
Desire to be part of a mission-driven team that values trust, humility, rigor, and creative ambition
An understanding of Okta’s business and experience working with cloud companies 
Social media experience and an analytical approach, with the ability to quickly identify trending topics and issues with a defined point of view
This role will be based in Okta’s San Francisco office and will be required to be in office
BS/BA degree

(P5571_3446150)

To learn more about our Total Rewards program please visit: https://rewards.okta.com/us.

Supporting Your Well-Being 
Driving Social Impact 
Developing Talent and Fostering Connection + Community

We are intentional about connection. Our global community, spanning over 20 offices worldwide, is united by a drive to innovate. Your journey begins with an immersive, in-person onboarding experience designed to accelerate your impact and connect you to our mission and team from day one.

jobs.title: Executive Director, CRE Transaction Management and Underwriting
jobs.description:
It Starts Here

Santander is a global leader and innovator in the financial services industry and is evolving from a high-impact brand into a technology-driven organization. Our people are at the heart of this journey and together, we are driving a customer-centric transformation that values bold thinking, innovation, and the courage to challenge what’s possible. This is more than a strategic shift. It’s a chance for driven professionals to grow, learn, and make a real difference.

If you are interested in exploring the possibilities We Want to Talk to You!

The Difference You Make

Santander Corporate & Investment Banking (SCIB) provides a comprehensive suite of financing, advisory, and banking solutions to corporate, financial sponsor, institutional investor, and commercial real estate clients globally. Within SCIB, Commercial Real Estate delivers integrated lending solutions to a diverse client base, including private equity sponsors, institutional investors, developers, REITs, and operating companies.

The Executive Director, Transaction Manager and Underwriter is a senior leader within the Commercial Real Estate platform, responsible for the underwriting, structuring, and overall deal execution of commercial real estate loan transactions across Santander's CMBS Conduit, Single-Asset Single-Borrower (SASB), Balance Sheet, and Warehouse lending platforms. This role serves as a player-coach, combining hands-on transaction leadership with mentorship and development of junior team members. The Executive Director partners closely with the Origination team, Credit Risk, Legal, Middle Office, Operations, and senior management to evaluate opportunities, manage risk, and deliver efficient deal execution from initial screening through loan closing, securitization, syndication, and asset management.

The successful candidate will bring deep expertise in commercial real estate credit, structured finance, and CMBS execution while contributing to the continued growth, profitability, and market presence of Santander's growing Commercial Real Estate platform.

Essential Duties And Responsibilities

Lead the underwriting, structuring, and execution of CMBS conduit, SASB, Balance Sheet, and Warehouse loan transactions across a broad range of property types and markets throughout the US.
Evaluate borrower submissions, property cash flows, sponsorship, market fundamentals, and third-party due diligence to determine transaction viability and creditworthiness.
Work with the team to structure loan proceeds, pricing, and terms consistent with Santander's risk appetite, credit standards, and profitability objectives.
Prepare and present transaction recommendations to internal credit, investment, and approval committees.
Serve as the primary execution lead throughout the transaction lifecycle, coordinating internal and external stakeholders to ensure timely and successful closings.
Oversee due diligence efforts, including appraisal, engineering, environmental, insurance, legal, and market reviews, both internally and with third-party vendor support relationships.
Coordinate with investors, B-piece buyers, rating agencies, legal counsel, Middle Office, and Operations teams to address transaction-related diligence and closing requirements.
Identify, assess, and mitigate transaction risks while balancing client needs, execution certainty, and portfolio objectives.
Monitor active pipeline activity, closing forecasts, and transaction metrics to support business planning and production goals.
Provide market intelligence and strategic insights regarding commercial real estate fundamentals, lending conditions, and competitive market dynamics.
Act as a player-coach by mentoring, training, and developing junior underwriting and execution professionals.
Contribute to the development and enhancement of underwriting standards, execution processes, and risk management practices.
Maintain current knowledge of commercial real estate market trends, CMBS activity, and industry developments.

Qualifications

Education

Bachelor's Degree in Finance, Real Estate, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field required.

Experience & Skills

10+ years of commercial real estate lending, CMBS, structured finance, or related experience.
Significant experience underwriting and executing across CMBS conduit/SASB/Balance Sheet transactions.
Strong understanding of CMBS securitization, rating agency methodologies, and institutional investor requirements.
Advanced financial modeling, cash flow analysis, and commercial real estate credit underwriting skills.
Demonstrated leadership experience managing deal teams and mentoring junior professionals.
Proven ability to manage multiple complex transactions in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.
Strong project management, organizational, and analytical capabilities.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills with the ability to present complex credit matters to senior stakeholders.
Proven ability to influence stakeholders and drive consensus across multiple business functions.
Strong relationship management skills with both internal and external stakeholders.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience across multiple commercial real estate asset classes, including office, multifamily, industrial, retail, hospitality, and specialty sectors.
Familiarity with loan securitization, warehouse financing, and institutional execution processes.
Established reputation for sound credit judgment and transaction execution within the commercial real estate industry.

What Else You Need To Know

The base pay range for this position is posted below and represents the annualized salary range. For hourly positions (non-exempt), the annual range is based on a 40-hour work week. The exact compensation may vary based on skills, experience, training, licensure and certifications and location.

Minimum

Base Pay Range:

$270,000.00 USD

Maximum

$330,000.00 USD

We Value Your Impact

Your contribution matters and it’s recognized. You can expect a fair and competitive rewards package that reflects the impact you create and the value you deliver. We know rewards go beyond numbers. Offering more than just a paycheck our benefits are designed to support you, your family and your well-being, now and into the future. Santander Benefits - 2026 Santander OnGoing/NH eGuide (foleon.com)

Risk Culture

We embrace a strong risk culture and all of our professionals at all levels are expected to take a proactive and responsible approach toward risk management.

EEO Statement

At Santander, we value and respect differences in our workforce. We actively encourage everyone to apply. Santander is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, genetics, disability, age, veteran status or any other characteristic protected by law.

Working Conditions

Frequent minimal physical effort such as sitting, standing and walking is required for this role. Depending on location, occasional moving and lifting light equipment and/or furniture may be required.

Employer Rights

This job description does not list all of the job duties of the job. You may be asked by your supervisors or managers to perform other duties. You may be evaluated in part based upon your performance of the tasks listed in this job description. The employer has the right to revise this job description at any time. This job description is not a contract for employment and either you or the employer may terminate your employment at any time for any reason.

What To Do Next

If this sounds like a role you are interested in, then please apply.

We are committed to providing an inclusive and accessible application process for all candidates. If you require any assistance or accommodation due to a disability or any other reason, please contact us at TAOps@santander.us to discuss your needs.

jobs.title: Program Director
jobs.description:
Company Description
Transcend NYC is a recovery-focused organization offering a recovery residence, a strong alumni program, and comprehensive peer support services. The recovery residence is a luxury five-story brownstone located in New York City’s historic Upper West Side, providing men with safe, supportive housing during their recovery journeys. Clients benefit from individualized peer services, working 1-on-1 with experienced recovery coaches and case managers. These professionals draw on their own recovery experience to help clients navigate resources, build stability, and sustain long-term wellness. Transcend NYC fosters a community-centered environment that emphasizes accountability, compassion, and personal growth.

Role Description The Program Director is a full-time, on-site role at our locatuon on the Upper West Side of Manhattan, responsible for overseeing the daily operations and quality of services within the recovery residence and associated programs. This role manages program staffing, scheduling, and supervision to ensure consistent, client-centered support and adherence to organizational policies and regulatory standards. The Program Director collaborates with recovery coaches, case managers, and other team members to design and refine programming, monitor client progress, and address individual needs. Daily responsibilities include coordinating intakes and discharges, maintaining accurate documentation, and ensuring clear communication with clients, families, and external partners. The position also involves continuous program evaluation, implementing improvements, and supporting a safe, structured environment aligned with Transcend NYC’s mission an
d values. Qualifications

Program management and leadership skills, including experience overseeing residential or behavioral health programs, supervising staff, and coordinating daily operations.
Client services and case management skills, such as conducting assessments, developing support plans, monitoring progress, and collaborating with multidisciplinary teams.
Communication and relationship-building skills, including the ability to communicate clearly with clients, families, staff, and community partners, and to handle sensitive information with discretion.
Crisis management and problem-solving skills, with the capacity to respond calmly to emergencies, resolve conflicts, and make sound decisions in fast-paced environments.
Knowledge of recovery-oriented care, substance use and mental health services, and best practices in peer support and supportive housing.
Strong organizational and documentation skills, including maintaining accurate records, tracking program metrics, and supporting compliance with policies and applicable regulations.
Ability to work on-site in New York, NY, maintain a consistent full-time schedule, and foster a safe, inclusive, and non-judgmental environment for all residents.
Bachelor’s degree in social work, psychology, counseling, public health, or a related field; an advanced degree or relevant certifications (e.g., CASAC, peer specialist) are preferred.
Previous experience in recovery services, residential treatment a plus.

The MD, Cardmember Access and Experiences is responsible for designing and delivering experiences, perks and privileges for Citi cardmembers that differentiate Citi products and drive customer acquisition, engagement, and profitability. This role will provide strategic thought leadership and work closely with the Cards Products and Travel & Rewards teams in redefining key verticals like Travel , Dining, Entertainment, Lifestyle and other categories to ensure that the loyalty and benefit ecosystem is aligned to product strategy, positioning and economics to create competitive advantage.

The leader will be expected to have in-depth knowledge of the credit card industry and direct competitors' value propositions. The successful candidate must bring a combination of strategic and execution skills and expected to articulate Citi’s strategic priorities in this space, garnering support, buy-in from key stakeholders and be accountable for the customer experience and economics associated with these programs. This role is part of the USCC Executive leadership team and will be expected to build close partnerships across the team and contribute to and influence the broader Cards strategy.

Responsibilities:

Outline and execute the benefits strategy based on product strategy across various card tiers (mass, affluent, premium), reinforcing Citi's pillars of Trust, Flexibility, and Global Access.
Own the strategy, content, and customer experience (CX) for benefits education and awareness in Citi’s mobile app and Citi.com (e.g., Benefits Hub)
Develop and manage strategic partnerships in key verticals, including:
Travel (Lounges, Hotels, Global Entry, CLEAR, Concierge)
Dining (Value, access, events)
Entertainment (Sports, concerts, events)
Lifestyle (Health/Wellness, Streaming, Rideshare)
Insurance
Other categories (e.g., Apparel, Retail), including new merchant partnerships for Thank You Rewards program
Ensure customer value and economics align with Citi's overall product strategy and profitability
Lead benefits launch, execution and fulfillment
Evaluate Return on Investment (ROI), customer satisfaction, and utilization of benefits. Manage the benefits with a product and portfolio management mindset, including quarterly reviews, annual refresh cycles, and lifecycle management
Manage all aspects of relationships, including contract negotiations, partner expansion, and cross-franchise opportunities
Leverage existing contracts and proactively redefine, renegotiate, or amend agreements as necessary to foster deeper, mutually beneficial relationships
Drive the business through balanced risk management, strong control discipline, and flawless operational execution.
Maintain an external network of industry contacts for market and talent intelligence
Understand industry trends, the competitive landscape, and Citi's position within it
Drive a strong people leadership agenda and be a culture carrier. Ensure that we are positioned for success with the best talent management practices including but not limited to coaching, development and ensuring strong succession plans are in place throughout the organization
Drive a strong culture and a productive work environment where people thrive, learn and get recognized
Manage, develop, and deliver written and oral communications, presentations, and assessments to executive leadership and broader teams
Appropriately assess risk when making business decisions, considering the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients, and assets. This involves driving compliance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct, and business practices, escalating, managing, and reporting control issues with transparency, and effectively supervising the activity of others while ensuring accountability for maintaining these standards

15+ years of management experience with a strong focus in financial services
Extensive experience in product management, partnership and business development
Demonstrated financial acumen and P&L management skills
Strong people leadership and stakeholder management skills
Proven success in growing a business and building internal and external partnerships
Strong technical ability and understanding of cutting-edge and emerging technology
Demonstrated ability to transform consumer research into actionable plans.
Demonstrated success in managing internal and external partnerships and the ability to negotiate complex structures
Ability to identify partners, make C-Suite level connections, build business cases, gain senior leadership support and sponsorship, negotiate agreements, and effectively onboard new partners
Comfortable operating in a highly matrixed, complex, and commoditized environment

Bachelor's/University degree required.

Managerial experience is essential.

jobs.title: Founding Director of Philanthropy
jobs.description:
The Zachriel Neurosciences Foundation is seeking an experienced fundraising executive to join our founding leadership team. This is a unique opportunity to help launch an independent human treatment initiative focused on Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) and early-stage Alzheimer's disease. Working alongside Foundation leadership, you will play a pivotal role in securing philanthropic support for a program with the potential to improve the lives of patients and families.
We will be conducting an Independent Human Therapy Test which is expected to take about 3-6 months to complete depending on the patient and their starting condition. Patients will be initially tested using an approved FDA blood-based biomarker test, along with other standardized cognitive tests, to identity patients with cognitive conditions such as Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) and early-stage Alzheimer's. After identifying patients with these conditions and their starting base line, patients will be administered a triple combination medication treatment to address three main causes of MCI and early-stage Alzheimer's as discovered by Dr. Michael D'Andrea (see www.zachrielneurosciences.org for more information on Dr. D'Andrea's discoveries and his 2 published books on the subject).
One of the major attractions of the triple combination treatment therapy developed by Dr. D'Andrea is the fact it utilizes existing FDA approved medications that have been repurposed to address the 3 main causes he discovered. Test Early and Treat Early is key according to Dr. D'Andrea's discoveries before advanced Alzheimer's causes more damage to the human brain and body.
The independent human testing is expected to show results with patients either experiencing improvements in their cognitive condition by either slowing, stopping or preventing the advancement of the disease. The independent human testing will be conducted by the Clinical Research Philadelphia group, which has also conducted numerous beneficial independent studies for many national pharmaceutical companies. Patients will be tested after months of treatment again using an approved FDA biomarker blood test along with other standard cognitive tests to confirm there is definitive evidence of improvement in the patient's condition.
This is a founding leadership opportunity for an individual passionate about changing the future of Alzheimer's disease. Compensation will be a Performance-Based Bonus Program with Executive Growth Opportunity. As the initiative progresses and funding milestones are achieved, we will establish a mutually agreed-upon compensation for key contributors

We are a fast-growing, technology-forward professional services firm seeking a Director of Operations to partner directly with the Founder and help lead the company through its next stage of growth.

About the Role

This is not a traditional operations role. We are looking for someone who naturally thinks in systems, thrives on accountability, and enjoys building organizations that scale. You'll serve as the operational counterpart to a visionary founder, transforming ideas into execution while creating the structure, discipline, and consistency necessary for long-term growth. jobs.title: Managing Director, Public Policy
jobs.description:
About This Role

BlackRock has a long history of service as a trusted, expert resource to governments and policymakers around the world. Since the Government Affairs & Public Policy (GAPP) team was created in 2009, it has steadily grown, transformed, and established BlackRock as a respected advocate for investors and the millions of retirees we serve. In recent years, that work has extended to long-term challenges such as the retirement crisis, energy security, and investment in critical infrastructure.

This role is for a Managing Director on GAPP to help drive BlackRock’s legislative advocacy at the federal level in the US. This individual will be responsible for developing, prioritizing, tracking, and communicating BlackRock’s public policy priorities with Congress and the White House.

Key Responsibilities

Advance BlackRock’s US federal public policy priorities, collaborating closely with Legal & Compliance, Communications, and other teams across BlackRock to ensure alignment of views.
Lead engagement with federal policymakers, trade associations, and clients on BlackRock’s priority issues.
Review key legislation and regulation and provide technical recommendations.
Coordinate with global public policy colleagues to ensure a consistent approach across regions.
Engage internal stakeholders to understand the interests of BlackRock and our clients as they relate to public policy developments; enlist subject matter experts as appropriate; assist with the development and management of internal working groups.
Develop and review materials for engagement with policymakers.
Build and maintain relationships with key policymakers and staff.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree required; J.D. from a nationally recognized law school preferred
10+ years of work experience in government relations, financial policy, or financial regulation.
Excellent judgment and impeccable ethics; desire to succeed in a collaborative, demanding, innovative, entrepreneurial environment.
Exceptional political acumen, thorough understanding of federal public policy and ability to be represent BlackRock with all policy makers.
Excellent communication skills, both oral and written. Must be able to present complex concepts in a concise and understandable way.
Strong skills in PowerPoint, Excel, and Word.
Strong research and writing skills; strategic and creative thinking aptitude.
Exceptional organizational skills and keen attention to detail.

For Washington, DC Only the salary range for this position is USD$250,000.00 - USD$325,000.00 . Additionally, employees are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus, and benefits including healthcare, leave benefits, and retirement benefits. BlackRock operates a pay-for-performance compensation philosophy and your total compensation may vary based on role, location, and firm, department and individual performance.

Our Benefits

To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about.

Our hybrid work model

BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock.

Guidance on AI use for candidates

At BlackRock, AI has long been part of how we work – enhancing decision-making, improving operations, and helping us deliver better outcomes for clients. We encourage candidates to use AI thoughtfully to learn, prepare, and work more effectively; but during our interview process, we want to focus on getting to know you through your own experiences, thinking, and judgment. To support you, we’ve provided guidance
on when and how to use AI during our hiring process so you can approach each step with confidence and showcase your best self.

About BlackRock

At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress.

This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive.

To learn more about BlackRock, please visit Careers.BlackRock.com. We also encourage you to get to know us on LinkedIn, Instagram, YouTube, X, and TikTok.

BlackRock is proud to be an equal opportunity workplace. We are committed to equal employment opportunity to all applicants and existing employees, and we evaluate qualified applicants without regard to race, creed, color, national origin, sex (including pregnancy and gender identity/expression), sexual orientation, age, ancestry, physical or mental disability, marital status, political affiliation, religion, citizenship status, genetic information, veteran status, or any other basis protected under applicable federal, state, or local law. View the
EEOC’s Know Your Rights poster and its supplement
and the
pay transparency statement.

BlackRock is committed to full inclusion of all qualified individuals and to providing reasonable accommodations or job modifications for individuals with disabilities. If reasonable accommodation/adjustments are needed throughout the employment process, please email Disability.Assistance@blackrock.com. All requests are treated in line with our privacy policy
.

BlackRock will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest or conviction records in a manner consistent with the requirements of the law, including any applicable fair chance law.

jobs.title: Director of Operations
jobs.description:
Position Summary

Odyssey Systems is seeking a mission-focused Operations Director
to lead and deliver success across a portfolio of programs within our Aviation Business Unit. In this role, you will ensure programs are executed with precision and purpose, directly supporting aviation readiness and the warfighter mission. You will oversee Program Managers, Task Leads, and staff, driving operational excellence, fiscal accountability, and a culture of employee engagement. Success in this role means strengthening customer trust, fostering collaboration, and consistently delivering high-quality performance that advances mission objectives. You will also manage subcontractor relationships and work closely with our Business Development team to drive growth, expand capabilities, and position Odyssey as a trusted partner in enabling aviation superiority and warfighter readiness.

Contingent upon contract award***

Responsibilities

Duties include, but are not limited to:

Plan and execute projects and customer engagements to achieve client objectives, milestones, and program benchmarks.

Contract Execution

Serve as the primary liaison between Odyssey Operations leadership and customer leadership teams, including the PEO and functional staff.
Ensure staffing requirements are met by partnering with Talent Acquisition to attract, recruit, and retain high-performing team members.
Support direct contract needs, including acquisition tasks, funding obligations, and other customer-driven requirements.
Establish goals, benchmarks, and milestones; monitor progress; and provide timely reporting on performance, risks, and issues.
Build and maintain strong customer relationships through regular engagement with CORs and division leadership to share status, feedback, and solutions to emerging requirements.
Provide value-added recommendations to anticipate and resolve potential project deficiencies.
Manage subcontractor relationships and coordinate across service-level organizations to ensure effective communication and collaboration.
Oversee contract deliverables and financial accountability, including monthly status reports, vacancy CDRLs, and cost tracking against budgets.

Employee Relations

Provide guidance, coaching, and feedback to support employee development and align goals with project and organizational objectives.
Identify and advocate for resources, training, and budget to meet development needs and strengthen team performance.
Support business growth by collaborating with Business Development on capture strategies, customer profiles, competitive analysis, and proposal activities.
Coordinate corporate resources and ensure consistent communication and messaging across teams, customers, and subcontractors.
Maintain regular engagement with employees and customers on program performance, resource needs, and business opportunities.

Leadership Attributes & Competencies

Proven record of leading and developing high-performing teams, managing staff across programs and functions to deliver measurable results.
Sets clear priorities and conveys objectives effectively, ensuring accountability while empowering employees through guidance, feedback, and professional development opportunities.
Demonstrates ethical leadership, integrity, and sound judgment, creating a positive and collaborative work environment built on trust and respect.
Skilled at building and maintaining strong customer relationships, engaging directly with government clients, understanding their priorities, and ensuring high-quality contract execution.
Navigates complex organizations with agility, leveraging formal and informal networks to influence outcomes and strengthen partnerships.
Communicates with clarity and impact in diverse settings, from small teams to senior executives, and consistently represents the organization professionally.
Addresses conflict directly and constructively, while recruiting, retaining, and developing top talent to support long-term business growth.
Highly action-oriented, bringing energy, persistence, and resilience to drive results, exceed goals, and inspire teams with vision and purpose.
Contingent upon contract award***

Qualifications

Citizenship: Must be a US citizen

Minimum Required Qualifications

Clearance:
Active Secret Clearance

Education:
Bachelor's Degree

Years of Experience: 10 plus years of comparable experience within the mission set, including previous senior management experience in the delivery of technical and management services (Program Management and Operations execution)

Education

Preferred Qualifications:

Master’s degree in business or another relevant field Technical Skills

Ability to work effectively with a broad range of people with a wide variety of technical skills and experience.
Ability to react quickly in a rapidly changing environment, motivate and manage a team, and support staff
Ability/experience managing professional personnel in a defense environment.
Ability/experience managing contract execution in a defense environment (hours, vacancies, funding, performance, quality)
Ability/experience managing contract financial execution/tracking/expenditures in a defense environment.
Ability/experience interfacing regularly with Government clients (Military and Civilian) on all aspects of contract execution.

Interpersonal Skills

Strong Leadership skills and experience
Experience leading portfolios of 100+ employees
Strong organization skills and ability to manage shifting priorities
Excellent interpersonal skills
Strong communication skills with the ability to speak to all levels of the organization, as well as external customers
Strong customer relationship building and management skills

Additional Information

Work location will be in Robins AFB, GA area

new_jobs.title: Managing Director, Mergers & Acquisitions
new_jobs.description:
In a world of disruption and increasingly complex business challenges, our professionals bring truth into focus with the Kroll Lens. Our sharp analytical skills, paired with the latest technology, allow us to give our clients clarity—not just answers—in all areas of business. We value the diverse backgrounds and perspectives that enable us to think globally. As part of One team, One Kroll , you’ll contribute to a supportive and collaborative work environment that empowers you to excel.

Kroll’s M&A Advisory service line is a leading middle-market M&A advisor. We advise public corporations, financial sponsors, family-owned businesses and other private companies in middle-market buy-side and sell-side M&A transactions globally, with regional teams situated in U.S., Canada, UK, Germany, Ireland, France, China, Brazil and India. Kroll has extensive experience in buy-side and sell-side engagements, capital raising, transaction advisory services and financial sponsor coverage.

At Kroll, your work will help deliver clarity to our clients’ most complex governance, risk and transparency challenges. Apply now to join One team, One Kroll .

Responsibilities

We have developed our expertise through extensive work in sell-side, buy-side and divestiture assignments on behalf of portfolio companies of private equity funds, closely held businesses and publicly traded companies.

Work on a variety of transactions in all stages, from initial client pitches to transaction closings
Communicates regulatory, financial information and complex strategic issues in a clear, concise and relevant manner to assist and guide decision-makers in corporate finance transactions
Ability to market services both to internal partners and external clients, develop client networks, and support efforts in developing and executing sales and marketing strategies
Ensure quality of client deliverables by having a strong attention to detail
Mentor and develop Staff, Vice Presidents and Directors
Individuals will be assigned a wide variety of projects and given as much responsibility as their experience and capabilities permit

Requirements

Minimum 10 years’ experience at a middle market, reputable boutique or regional investment bank serving the middle market with demonstrable M&A execution experience.
Bachelor's, Master's or MBA degree
Ability to generate new business through the cultivation of existing relationships as well as the development of new relationships by various efforts including cold calling.
Ability to cross-sell other firm services within Corporate Finance and our other business units of GRA, Cyber, VAS and Kroll Business Services.
Ability to make effective decisions by analyzing information and considering priorities
Proficient in MS Office, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint
Demonstrated experience with managing of day to day aspects of client relationships and projects
Demonstrated record of leadership and effective management in matrixed organizations
Excellent written and verbal communication skills that help represent diverse communities
Experience working with diverse teams

In order to be considered for a position, you must formally apply via careers.kroll.com.

Kroll is committed to creating an inclusive work environment. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and will consider all qualified applicants regardless of gender, gender identity, race, religion, color, nationality, ethnic origin, sexual orientation, marital status, veteran status, age or disability.

new_jobs.title: CEO Communications Manager
new_jobs.description:
Secure Every Identity, from AI to Human

Identity is the key to unlocking the potential of AI. Okta secures AI by building the trusted, neutral infrastructure that enables organizations to safely embrace this new era. This work requires a relentless drive to solve complex challenges with real-world stakes. We are looking for builders and owners who operate with speed and urgency and execute with excellence.

This is an opportunity to do career-defining work. We're all in on this mission. If you are too, let's talk.
Title:
CEO Communications Manager
San Francisco (in-office required) 
The CEO Communications Manager opportunity

We’re seeking a high-performing CEO Communications Manager to help amplify the voice and vision of our CEO and drive AI optimization across his communications channels. In this role, you will report to Okta’s Head of CEO Communications and partner closely with the Office of the CEO to support, streamline, and scale the CEO comms platform, spanning internal and external speaking events, media & influencer engagements, and social media. You will also work with the Corporate Communications team and partner with the broader executive bench, cross-functional partners, and our various agencies to develop bylines, blog posts, keynotes, talking points, and thought leadership content.
This is a rare opportunity to sit at the intersection of content, operations, strategy, and execution—all in support of a visionary founder leading a global tech company through transformation and growth. The ideal candidate is intellectually curious, highly accountable, and capable of grasping both business strategy as well as the underlying technology, particularly in the context of identity security, AI, and enterprise software. 
What you’ll be doing 

Content development:
Develop and refine bylines, scripts, talking points, social copy, and briefing materials for internal and external engagements that faithfully reflect the CEO’s vision and voice across audiences ranging from employees and customers to global media and boards of directors.
Data driven optimization:
Establish and maintain rigorous feedback loops and use engagement, sentiment, and reach data to continuously refine the CEO’s communication strategy, with an eye toward emerging trends in identity, AI, and enterprise tech.
Agentic innovation:
Continuously
apply and explore how AI and agents can securely optimize and automate workflows and content development across the CEO’s communications platform.
Event planning & production support:
Serve as point of contact for production logistics across company events, including tech run-throughs, AV coordination, studio operations, and real-time event support.
Stakeholder management:
Design and own streamlined, scalable workflows for the end-to-end content lifecycle—from ideation and drafting through cross-functional, legal, and PR review, to distribution. Manage inputs, timelines, and deliverables across multiple stakeholders, often coordinating between executives and production/design teams to ensure alignment across all CEO materials.
Operational excellence:
The ideal candidate will thrive in and derive energy from a high-pressure, high-performing environment. Obsessive quality control and meticulous attention to detail are table stakes. You will  
anticipate needs, respond quickly, and act as a strategic thought partner—particularly in high-stakes, fast-paced settings. A bias for optimization and innovation will be highly valued.

What you’ll bring to the role & what we’re looking for

5-7+ years of experience in corporate communications including direct experience with executive and/or CEO communications, ideally in a high-growth tech or SaaS company
Experience writing with and for CEOs, executives, leaders, and/or founders, with the ability to adapt to and maintain a distinct executive tone of voice
Outstanding written and verbal communication skills, with high attention to detail
Experience with and eagerness to apply AI to enhance and reimagine processes while improving content quality and speed
A passion for operational excellence and ability to thrive in a high-performance environment, taking initiative, owning outcomes, and operating with polish and discretion
Strong project management instincts—capable of juggling shifting priorities, tight timelines, and multiple stakeholders with calm and precision
Desire to be part of a mission-driven team that values trust, humility, rigor, and creative ambition
An understanding of Okta’s business and experience working with cloud companies 
Social media experience and an analytical approach, with the ability to quickly identify trending topics and issues with a defined point of view
This role will be based in Okta’s San Francisco office and will be required to be in office
BS/BA degree

new_jobs.title: Executive Director, CRE Transaction Management and Underwriting
new_jobs.description:
It Starts Here

Santander is a global leader and innovator in the financial services industry and is evolving from a high-impact brand into a technology-driven organization. Our people are at the heart of this journey and together, we are driving a customer-centric transformation that values bold thinking, innovation, and the courage to challenge what’s possible. This is more than a strategic shift. It’s a chance for driven professionals to grow, learn, and make a real difference.

If you are interested in exploring the possibilities We Want to Talk to You!

The Difference You Make

Santander Corporate & Investment Banking (SCIB) provides a comprehensive suite of financing, advisory, and banking solutions to corporate, financial sponsor, institutional investor, and commercial real estate clients globally. Within SCIB, Commercial Real Estate delivers integrated lending solutions to a diverse client base, including private equity sponsors, institutional investors, developers, REITs, and operating companies.

The Executive Director, Transaction Manager and Underwriter is a senior leader within the Commercial Real Estate platform, responsible for the underwriting, structuring, and overall deal execution of commercial real estate loan transactions across Santander's CMBS Conduit, Single-Asset Single-Borrower (SASB), Balance Sheet, and Warehouse lending platforms. This role serves as a player-coach, combining hands-on transaction leadership with mentorship and development of junior team members. The Executive Director partners closely with the Origination team, Credit Risk, Legal, Middle Office, Operations, and senior management to evaluate opportunities, manage risk, and deliver efficient deal execution from initial screening through loan closing, securitization, syndication, and asset management.

The successful candidate will bring deep expertise in commercial real estate credit, structured finance, and CMBS execution while contributing to the continued growth, profitability, and market presence of Santander's growing Commercial Real Estate platform.

Essential Duties And Responsibilities

Lead the underwriting, structuring, and execution of CMBS conduit, SASB, Balance Sheet, and Warehouse loan transactions across a broad range of property types and markets throughout the US.
Evaluate borrower submissions, property cash flows, sponsorship, market fundamentals, and third-party due diligence to determine transaction viability and creditworthiness.
Work with the team to structure loan proceeds, pricing, and terms consistent with Santander's risk appetite, credit standards, and profitability objectives.
Prepare and present transaction recommendations to internal credit, investment, and approval committees.
Serve as the primary execution lead throughout the transaction lifecycle, coordinating internal and external stakeholders to ensure timely and successful closings.
Oversee due diligence efforts, including appraisal, engineering, environmental, insurance, legal, and market reviews, both internally and with third-party vendor support relationships.
Coordinate with investors, B-piece buyers, rating agencies, legal counsel, Middle Office, and Operations teams to address transaction-related diligence and closing requirements.
Identify, assess, and mitigate transaction risks while balancing client needs, execution certainty, and portfolio objectives.
Monitor active pipeline activity, closing forecasts, and transaction metrics to support business planning and production goals.
Provide market intelligence and strategic insights regarding commercial real estate fundamentals, lending conditions, and competitive market dynamics.
Act as a player-coach by mentoring, training, and developing junior underwriting and execution professionals.
Contribute to the development and enhancement of underwriting standards, execution processes, and risk management practices.
Maintain current knowledge of commercial real estate market trends, CMBS activity, and industry developments.

Qualifications

Education

Bachelor's Degree in Finance, Real Estate, Economics, Business Administration, or a related field required.

Experience & Skills

10+ years of commercial real estate lending, CMBS, structured finance, or related experience.
Significant experience underwriting and executing across CMBS conduit/SASB/Balance Sheet transactions.
Strong understanding of CMBS securitization, rating agency methodologies, and institutional investor requirements.
Advanced financial modeling, cash flow analysis, and commercial real estate credit underwriting skills.
Demonstrated leadership experience managing deal teams and mentoring junior professionals.
Proven ability to manage multiple complex transactions in a fast-paced, deadline-driven environment.
Strong project management, organizational, and analytical capabilities.
Excellent verbal and written communication skills with the ability to present complex credit matters to senior stakeholders.
Proven ability to influence stakeholders and drive consensus across multiple business functions.
Strong relationship management skills with both internal and external stakeholders.

Preferred Qualifications

Experience across multiple commercial real estate asset classes, including office, multifamily, industrial, retail, hospitality, and specialty sectors.
Familiarity with loan securitization, warehouse financing, and institutional execution processes.
Established reputation for sound credit judgment and transaction execution within the commercial real estate industry.

What Else You Need To Know

The base pay range for this position is posted below and represents the annualized salary range. For hourly positions (non-exempt), the annual range is based on a 40-hour work week. The exact compensation may vary based on skills, experience, training, licensure and certifications and location.

Minimum

Base Pay Range:

$270,000.00 USD

Maximum

$330,000.00 USD

We Value Your Impact

Your contribution matters and it’s recognized. You can expect a fair and competitive rewards package that reflects the impact you create and the value you deliver. We know rewards go beyond numbers. Offering more than just a paycheck our benefits are designed to support you, your family and your well-being, now and into the future. Santander Benefits - 2026 Santander OnGoing/NH eGuide (foleon.com)

Risk Culture

We embrace a strong risk culture and all of our professionals at all levels are expected to take a proactive and responsible approach toward risk management.

At Santander, we value and respect differences in our workforce. We actively encourage everyone to apply. Santander is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, genetics, disability, age, veteran status or any other characteristic protected by law.

Working Conditions

new_jobs.title: Program Director
new_jobs.description:
Company Description
Transcend NYC is a recovery-focused organization offering a recovery residence, a strong alumni program, and comprehensive peer support services. The recovery residence is a luxury five-story brownstone located in New York City’s historic Upper West Side, providing men with safe, supportive housing during their recovery journeys. Clients benefit from individualized peer services, working 1-on-1 with experienced recovery coaches and case managers. These professionals draw on their own recovery experience to help clients navigate resources, build stability, and sustain long-term wellness. Transcend NYC fosters a community-centered environment that emphasizes accountability, compassion, and personal growth.

Role Description The Program Director is a full-time, on-site role at our locatuon on the Upper West Side of Manhattan, responsible for overseeing the daily operations and quality of services within the recovery residence and associated programs. This role manages program staffing, scheduling, and supervision to ensure consistent, client-centered support and adherence to organizational policies and regulatory standards. The Program Director collaborates with recovery coaches, case managers, and other team members to design and refine programming, monitor client progress, and address individual needs. Daily responsibilities include coordinating intakes and discharges, maintaining accurate documentation, and ensuring clear communication with clients, families, and external partners. The position also involves continuous program evaluation, implementing improvements, and supporting a safe, structured environment aligned with Transcend NYC’s mission an
d values. Qualifications

Program management and leadership skills, including experience overseeing residential or behavioral health programs, supervising staff, and coordinating daily operations.
Client services and case management skills, such as conducting assessments, developing support plans, monitoring progress, and collaborating with multidisciplinary teams.
Communication and relationship-building skills, including the ability to communicate clearly with clients, families, staff, and community partners, and to handle sensitive information with discretion.
Crisis management and problem-solving skills, with the capacity to respond calmly to emergencies, resolve conflicts, and make sound decisions in fast-paced environments.
Knowledge of recovery-oriented care, substance use and mental health services, and best practices in peer support and supportive housing.
Strong organizational and documentation skills, including maintaining accurate records, tracking program metrics, and supporting compliance with policies and applicable regulations.
Ability to work on-site in New York, NY, maintain a consistent full-time schedule, and foster a safe, inclusive, and non-judgmental environment for all residents.
Bachelor’s degree in social work, psychology, counseling, public health, or a related field; an advanced degree or relevant certifications (e.g., CASAC, peer specialist) are preferred.
Previous experience in recovery services, residential treatment a plus.

new_jobs.description:

Industry/Sector:

The Opportunity

As a Thought Leadership Survey Development Director, you will play a pivotal role in shaping the future of industries by creating unique perspectives and insights on overarching themes and industry-specific subjects. Within our Internal Firm Services practice, you will drive strategic sales and marketing initiatives, enhancing brand visibility and capturing new business opportunities. By utilizing market research, digital marketing, and creative campaigns, you will engage clients and promote the firm's services.

As a Director, you will set the strategic direction and lead business growth efforts. You will make impactful decisions and oversee multiple projects, maintaining executive-level relations. Translating the vision, you will set the tone and inspire others to follow. Your role is crucial in driving business growth, shaping the direction of projects, and mentoring the next generation of leaders. You are expected to be a guardian of PwC's reputation, understanding that quality, integrity, inclusion, and a commercial mindset are all foundational to our success.

In this role, you will develop new ideas, solutions, and structures, driving thought leadership. You will solve problems by exploring multiple angles and using creativity, encouraging others to do the same. You will also promote technological advances, creating an environment where people and technology thrive together.

Responsibilities

Developing and executing strategic sales and marketing initiatives to drive revenue growth and enhance brand visibility
Creating unique perspectives and insights on industry-specific subjects to position PwC as trusted advisors
Collaborating with teams to frame distinctive points of view that shape the future of industries
Utilizing market research and digital marketing strategies to capture new business opportunities
Promoting the Firm's services through creative campaigns and effective sales strategies
Conducting research and analysis to inform content creation and development
Leading thought leadership efforts by developing new ideas, solutions, and structures
Managing content quality and optimization to validate impactful communication
Identifying gaps in the market and converting opportunities into success for the Firm
Cultivating potential in others and mentoring the next generation of leaders

What You Must Have

At least a High School Diploma or the equivalent degree
At least 8 years of experience in sales, marketing or PwC experience

What Sets You Apart

Preference for a Bachelor's degree
Demonstrating strategic leadership in thought leadership initiatives
Excelling in content creation and content strategy
Utilizing data analytics for market insights and decision-making
Leading survey design and survey research projects
Promoting innovation and creativity in content development
Cultivating potential through coaching and feedback

The salary range for this position is: $122,500 - $423,780. For residents of Washington state the salary range for this position is: $122,500 - $504,500. Actual compensation within the range will be dependent upon the individual's skills, experience, qualifications and location, and applicable employment laws. All hired individuals are eligible for an annual discretionary bonus. new_jobs.title: Managing Director, Head of Cardmember Access & Experiences, US Consumer Cards
new_jobs.description:
The MD, Cardmember Access and Experiences is responsible for designing and delivering experiences, perks and privileges for Citi cardmembers that differentiate Citi products and drive customer acquisition, engagement, and profitability. This role will provide strategic thought leadership and work closely with the Cards Products and Travel & Rewards teams in redefining key verticals like Travel , Dining, Entertainment, Lifestyle and other categories to ensure that the loyalty and benefit ecosystem is aligned to product strategy, positioning and economics to create competitive advantage.

The leader will be expected to have in-depth knowledge of the credit card industry and direct competitors' value propositions. The successful candidate must bring a combination of strategic and execution skills and expected to articulate Citi’s strategic priorities in this space, garnering support, buy-in from key stakeholders and be accountable for the customer experience and economics associated with these programs. This role is part of the USCC Executive leadership team and will be expected to build close partnerships across the team and contribute to and influence the broader Cards strategy.

Responsibilities:

Outline and execute the benefits strategy based on product strategy across various card tiers (mass, affluent, premium), reinforcing Citi's pillars of Trust, Flexibility, and Global Access.
Own the strategy, content, and customer experience (CX) for benefits education and awareness in Citi’s mobile app and Citi.com (e.g., Benefits Hub)
Develop and manage strategic partnerships in key verticals, including:
Travel (Lounges, Hotels, Global Entry, CLEAR, Concierge)
Dining (Value, access, events)
Entertainment (Sports, concerts, events)
Lifestyle (Health/Wellness, Streaming, Rideshare)
Insurance
Other categories (e.g., Apparel, Retail), including new merchant partnerships for Thank You Rewards program
Ensure customer value and economics align with Citi's overall product strategy and profitability
Lead benefits launch, execution and fulfillment
Evaluate Return on Investment (ROI), customer satisfaction, and utilization of benefits. Manage the benefits with a product and portfolio management mindset, including quarterly reviews, annual refresh cycles, and lifecycle management
Manage all aspects of relationships, including contract negotiations, partner expansion, and cross-franchise opportunities
Leverage existing contracts and proactively redefine, renegotiate, or amend agreements as necessary to foster deeper, mutually beneficial relationships
Drive the business through balanced risk management, strong control discipline, and flawless operational execution.
Maintain an external network of industry contacts for market and talent intelligence
Understand industry trends, the competitive landscape, and Citi's position within it
Drive a strong people leadership agenda and be a culture carrier. Ensure that we are positioned for success with the best talent management practices including but not limited to coaching, development and ensuring strong succession plans are in place throughout the organization
Drive a strong culture and a productive work environment where people thrive, learn and get recognized
Manage, develop, and deliver written and oral communications, presentations, and assessments to executive leadership and broader teams
Appropriately assess risk when making business decisions, considering the firm's reputation and safeguarding Citigroup, its clients, and assets. This involves driving compliance with applicable laws, rules, and regulations, adhering to Policy, applying sound ethical judgment regarding personal behavior, conduct, and business practices, escalating, managing, and reporting control issues with transparency, and effectively supervising the activity of others while ensuring accountability for maintaining these standards

Qualifications:

15+ years of management experience with a strong focus in financial services
Extensive experience in product management, partnership and business development
Demonstrated financial acumen and P&L management skills
Strong people leadership and stakeholder management skills
Proven success in growing a business and building internal and external partnerships
Strong technical ability and understanding of cutting-edge and emerging technology
Demonstrated ability to transform consumer research into actionable plans.
Demonstrated success in managing internal and external partnerships and the ability to negotiate complex structures
Ability to identify partners, make C-Suite level connections, build business cases, gain senior leadership support and sponsorship, negotiate agreements, and effectively onboard new partners
Comfortable operating in a highly matrixed, complex, and commoditized environment

Education:

Bachelor's/University degree required.
Master's degree preferred.
Managerial experience is essential.

new_jobs.title: Founding Director of Philanthropy
new_jobs.description:
The Zachriel Neurosciences Foundation is seeking an experienced fundraising executive to join our founding leadership team. This is a unique opportunity to help launch an independent human treatment initiative focused on Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) and early-stage Alzheimer's disease. Working alongside Foundation leadership, you will play a pivotal role in securing philanthropic support for a program with the potential to improve the lives of patients and families.
We will be conducting an Independent Human Therapy Test which is expected to take about 3-6 months to complete depending on the patient and their starting condition. Patients will be initially tested using an approved FDA blood-based biomarker test, along with other standardized cognitive tests, to identity patients with cognitive conditions such as Mild Cognitive Impairment (MCI) and early-stage Alzheimer's. After identifying patients with these conditions and their starting base line, patients will be administered a triple combination medication treatment to address three main causes of MCI and early-stage Alzheimer's as discovered by Dr. Michael D'Andrea (see www.zachrielneurosciences.org for more information on Dr. D'Andrea's discoveries and his 2 published books on the subject).
One of the major attractions of the triple combination treatment therapy developed by Dr. D'Andrea is the fact it utilizes existing FDA approved medications that have been repurposed to address the 3 main causes he discovered. Test Early and Treat Early is key according to Dr. D'Andrea's discoveries before advanced Alzheimer's causes more damage to the human brain and body.
The independent human testing is expected to show results with patients either experiencing improvements in their cognitive condition by either slowing, stopping or preventing the advancement of the disease. The independent human testing will be conducted by the Clinical Research Philadelphia group, which has also conducted numerous beneficial independent studies for many national pharmaceutical companies. Patients will be tested after months of treatment again using an approved FDA biomarker blood test along with other standard cognitive tests to confirm there is definitive evidence of improvement in the patient's condition.
This is a founding leadership opportunity for an individual passionate about changing the future of Alzheimer's disease. Compensation will be a Performance-Based Bonus Program with Executive Growth Opportunity. As the initiative progresses and funding milestones are achieved, we will establish a mutually agreed-upon compensation for key contributors

new_jobs.title: Director of Operations
new_jobs.description:
About the Company

We are a fast-growing, technology-forward professional services firm seeking a Director of Operations to partner directly with the Founder and help lead the company through its next stage of growth.

About the Role

This is not a traditional operations role. We are looking for someone who naturally thinks in systems, thrives on accountability, and enjoys building organizations that scale. You'll serve as the operational counterpart to a visionary founder, transforming ideas into execution while creating the structure, discipline, and consistency necessary for long-term growth. If you enjoy creating order from complexity, improving people and processes, and building high-performing teams, we'd like to speak with you.

Responsibilities

Operational Leadership

Lead daily business operations across all departments.
Build scalable systems and standardized operating procedures.
Improve organizational structure and operational efficiency.
Ensure consistent execution of company priorities.
Drive accountability across the leadership team.

Process Improvement

Evaluate current workflows and eliminate inefficiencies.
Establish and maintain operational scorecards and KPIs.
Improve project management execution and visibility.
Implement systems that increase consistency and predictability.
Identify opportunities to leverage automation and AI throughout the business.

Leadership & Accountability

Coach managers on performance expectations and accountability.
Hold leaders accountable to commitments and measurable outcomes.
Drive a culture of ownership and continuous improvement.
Address performance issues proactively and professionally.
Lead difficult conversations with clarity and consistency.

Strategic Execution

Partner directly with the Founder to prioritize initiatives.
Translate strategic vision into executable plans.
Ensure cross-functional alignment across the organization.
Create operational capacity that allows leadership to focus on growth rather than day-to-day management.

Client & Business Excellence

Improve client delivery processes and quality control.
Strengthen project oversight and customer experience.
Monitor operational metrics and proactively address issues before they impact clients.
Develop systems that increase enterprise value and scalability.

Qualifications

Experience building systems in growing entrepreneurial or founder-led organizations.
Strong understanding of operational metrics and business performance.
Proven success leading organizational change.
Experience implementing EOS or similar operating systems is highly preferred.
Excellent communication and leadership skills.
High emotional intelligence and sound judgment.
Bachelor's degree preferred.

Strong understanding of operational metrics and business performance.

Excellent communication and leadership skills.

Preferred Skills

Experience implementing EOS or similar operating systems is highly preferred.
High emotional intelligence and sound judgment.

$140,000 Base Salary + Performance-Based Bonus

Equal Opportunity Statement

We are committed to diversity and inclusivity in our hiring practices.

At least a High School Diploma or the equivalent degree

Utilizing data analytics for market insights and decision-making
